summary: SQL injection via unsanitized input concatenated into SQL queries.
operatorGuidance: Confirm with error-based, time-based, and OAST payloads
Map all input parameters before probing
Use blind time-based confirmation if no error output is visible
Chain OAST callback to confirm blind injection without timing reliance

triggerFacts: Payload reflected in response body without encoding
summary: Reflected XSS via unsanitized user input echoed back into the HTML response.
operatorGuidance: Confirm payload executes in a real browser context
Identify the reflection context (HTML body, attribute, JS block, URL)
Test both GET and POST submission paths
Check CSP headers that may prevent exploitation

summary: XSS oracle classification confirms sanitisation bypass or encoding gap in XSS defences.
operatorGuidance: Run oracle payloads across all insertion points in the request
Classify responses using oracle response classifier
Escalate expected-proof classifications to reflected-xss confirmation
Document verifier-type-error responses as partial bypass evidence

triggerFacts: Template syntax reflected with evaluation result
Error message reveals template engine name
summary: Server-side template injection via user-controlled input passed into a template engine.
operatorGuidance: Use engine-agnostic polyglot payloads first to detect evaluation
Fingerprint template engine from error messages or evaluation results
Escalate to RCE via engine-specific payloads
Test Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker, Pebble, Velocity, Handlebars patterns
expectedProof: Mathematical expression result in response (e.g. 49 from 7*7)

summary: Local file inclusion via path traversal in a file parameter, allowing arbitrary file reads.
operatorGuidance: Start with /etc/passwd or Windows equivalents as low-risk proof files
Test URL-encoded and double-encoded traversal sequences
Check for null-byte termination bypasses
Escalate to RCE via log poisoning or /proc/self/fd if available

triggerFacts: OAST DNS callback from nslookup/curl payload
summary: OS command injection via unsanitized input passed to shell execution context.
operatorGuidance: Use time-based blind payloads first to avoid noisy output
Confirm with OAST DNS lookup for reliable out-of-band confirmation
Test semicolon, pipe, ampersand, and backtick separators
Identify the shell context (sh, bash, cmd, powershell)

summary: Server-side request forgery via user-controlled URL causing the server to make internal requests.
operatorGuidance: Probe cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254, 100.100.100.200)
Test loopback and RFC-1918 ranges
Use URL scheme variants: http, https, gopher, dict, file
Check for partial SSRF via redirect chains

summary: Blind SSRF confirmed via out-of-band DNS or HTTP callback to an OAST listener.
operatorGuidance: Use unique per-request OAST subdomains to correlate callbacks
Wait at least 30s for delayed callbacks
Test both HTTP and HTTPS probe URLs
Confirm the server-side user-agent in OAST logs for fingerprinting
expectedProof: OAST DNS callback with unique token
Correlation between request timestamp and callback

summary: Open redirect allows attacker-controlled redirection, enabling phishing and OAuth token theft.
operatorGuidance: Test absolute and relative URL payloads
Check for allowlist bypass via double-slash, at-sign, and fragment tricks
Assess severity in context of OAuth redirect_uri or SSO flows
Document redirect chain depth and final destination
expectedProof: HTTP 3xx response with Location to attacker domain
Browser navigation to external origin confirmed

triggerFacts: XML parsing error exposing entity processing
summary: XML external entity injection via malicious DOCTYPE in XML input processed by the server.
operatorGuidance: Inject SYSTEM entity pointing to /etc/passwd for in-band proof
Use parameter entities if external general entities are blocked
Test XInclude and XPath injection as alternatives
Escalate blind XXE via OAST HTTP callback
expectedProof: File contents in response via entity reference
SSRF to internal host via external entity

triggerFacts: OAST DNS callback from external entity URL
Blind XXE with no in-band output
summary: Blind XXE confirmed via out-of-band DNS/HTTP callback when no in-band error output is available.
operatorGuidance: Use parameter entity with external DTD reference to trigger OAST
Correlate OAST callback timing with request submission
Chain with file exfiltration via out-of-band data channel
Test both DNS and HTTP OOB channels for firewall traversal
expectedProof: OAST DNS callback with unique token
OAST HTTP request containing entity data

summary: NoSQL injection via operator injection in MongoDB, CouchDB, or similar document store queries.
operatorGuidance: Test $gt, $ne, $regex operators in JSON body and query params
Attempt auth bypass with {$ne: null} on password field
Use $where clause injection for JavaScript execution where supported
Fingerprint database type from error messages
expectedProof: NoSQL error message with operator syntax

triggerFacts: Injected Host value reflected in response
Password reset link contains attacker domain
Cache poisoning via Host header confirmed
summary: Host header injection allows cache poisoning, password reset hijacking, or internal routing abuse.
operatorGuidance: Test X-Forwarded-Host, X-Host, X-Original-Host as alternatives
Check password reset flows for Host-reflected links
Probe for routing differences when Host points to internal hostnames
Verify if the injected value is cached and served to other users
expectedProof: Injected Host value in response body or Location header
Password reset email with attacker-controlled domain
Cache hit serving poisoned response to other requests

summary: CRLF injection via \r\n sequences in user input allows header injection and response splitting.
operatorGuidance: Test URL-encoded %0d%0a in query parameters and path segments
Confirm injected headers appear in response
Assess session fixation risk via Set-Cookie injection
Check for XSS via injected Content-Type or body splitting
expectedProof: Injected header present in HTTP response
Set-Cookie header injected with attacker value
Response body content injected via splitting

triggerFacts: Poisoned response served from cache to other requests
Unkeyed input reflected in cached response
summary: Web cache poisoning via unkeyed request inputs that influence cached responses served to all users.
operatorGuidance: Identify unkeyed headers using Param Miner or equivalent
Confirm reflection of unkeyed input in response body
Verify poisoned entry is cached with X-Cache or Age headers
Assess XSS or redirect payload deliverable via cached response
expectedProof: Poisoned response returned from cache (X-Cache: HIT)
Unkeyed input reflected in cached response

triggerFacts: JWT algorithm confusion accepted (RS256 to HS256)
summary: JWT attack via algorithm confusion, none algorithm, or weak HMAC secret allowing token forgery.
operatorGuidance: Test none algorithm by stripping signature
Attempt RS256-to-HS256 confusion with public key as HMAC secret
Crack short or default HMAC secrets with jwt-cracker or hashcat
Test kid header injection for SQL or file path traversal
expectedProof: Elevated privileges granted with manipulated claims

summary: CORS misconfiguration allows cross-origin credential theft when the server reflects attacker-controlled origins.
operatorGuidance: Test null origin and arbitrary subdomain reflection
Confirm Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true is present
Assess impact based on what authenticated endpoints are reachable
Check pre-flight OPTIONS handling for method/header exposure
expectedProof: ACAC: true present alongside reflected origin
Cross-origin fetch of authenticated response succeeds

triggerFacts: Debug fields or internal types visible
summary: GraphQL introspection enabled in production exposes full schema including internal types and mutations.
operatorGuidance: Run introspection query to enumerate all types, fields, mutations
Map hidden or undocumented mutations for further probing
Test for field-level authorization gaps on sensitive resolvers
Probe for batching attacks and alias-based rate limit bypass

triggerFacts: Role or privilege field writable by user
summary: Mass assignment vulnerability allows users to set protected fields by sending unexpected parameters.
operatorGuidance: Add privileged fields (role, admin, isVerified) to update requests
Confirm field persistence by reading state after write
Compare API request schemas with database model definitions
Test nested object assignment for bypassed allowlists
expectedProof: Role escalation reflected in subsequent authenticated requests
Response body confirms assigned field value

triggerFacts: Authorization check absent on object lookup
summary: Insecure direct object reference allows access to other users' resources via manipulated identifiers.
operatorGuidance: Map all object identifiers in API endpoints (IDs, GUIDs, slugs)
Test cross-account access with a second test account
Probe horizontal and vertical privilege escalation paths
Check for GUID predictability or hash reversibility
expectedProof: Another user's data returned in response
Authorization boundary not enforced on object lookup
